notes: [mapobject-func.button | presentation] func_button — press-to-fire (touch/use/damage), wait+return, setactive timer, alt texture  -  the pressed-state alternate texture (Frame=1) is set on the entity but whether the brush model renders the alt frame in-engine is unverified (presentation).; CORRECTED 2026-06-25: button_reset round-restart re-arm is LIVE — ButtonReset is assigned at Buttons.cs:42 and dispatched by GameWorld.ResetMapObjects (GameWorld.cs:3887). The prior gap note claiming it was 'STILL dead' / the comment is wrong was stale; the Buttons.cs:15 comment is correct. (See mapobject-func.reset.)

[mapobject-func.plat | presentation] func_plat — riding platform (center trigger raises, 3s top dwell, crush/reverse)  -  plat_reset initial placement is live + faithful (targeted->raised, untargeted->bottom); the round-restart .reset is ALSO live now — PlatReset is assigned at Platforms.cs:82 and dispatched by GameWorld.ResetMapObjects. (CORRECTED 2026-06-25; the prior 'STILL NOT wired' note was stale — see mapobject-func.reset.); plat_outside_touch (the CSQC-side outside trigger that sends a top plat down when a player leaves) is not ported — it is gated to CSQC in Base; minor.; no CSQC ENT_CLIENT_PLAT draw/networking.

[mapobject-func.train | presentation,audio] func_train — path_corner chain ride (TURN/CURVE/NEEDACTIVATION/random, per-corner speed/wait)  -  no func_train reset (QC explicitly has `// TODO make a reset function for this one` — so this is faithful to Base's own omission, unlike door/plat/button).; train moving-sound stop (stopsoundto MSG_BROADCAST on arrival) is not done; the moving noise plays but is not explicitly stopped at each dwell (cosmetic). Port uses SoundChannel.Voice vs QC ATTEN_IDLE (cosmetic).; no CSQC ENT_CLIENT_TRAIN draw/networking.

[mapobject-func.bobbing | audio] func_bobbing — sine translation via 0.1s controller  -  the spawn-time soundto(MSG_INIT) looping ambient is not resent per-player; played through the facade where present.
[mapobject-func.pendulum | audio] func_pendulum — sine roll via 0.1s controller (Q3A freq formula)  -  ambient .noise not resent per-player (same reduction as bobbing).
[mapobject-func.fourier | audio] func_fourier — sum-of-sines mover (netname quintuple list)  -  looping ambient .noise resent per-player (soundto MSG_INIT) reduced to a single facade Play.
[mapobject-func.vectormamamam | audio] func_vectormamamam — 4-reference projected mover  -  looping ambient .noise per-player resend reduced to facade Play in setactive.

[assault.objective.activate | presentation] Activate an objective (set health, arm its decreasers + destructibles)  -  ActivateObjective also spawns the objective's waypoint sprite (SpawnObjectiveSprite → WP_AssaultDestroy + health bar) on the server, matching QC's WaypointSprite_SpawnFixed. But there is no client render/networking path, so the sprite is not visible on-screen (see assault.objective.waypoint_sprite). Logic/state is faithful; the visible marker is still absent.

[assault.objective.waypoint_sprite | presentation] Objective waypoint sprites (defend/push/destroy + health bars + radar icon)  -  Presentation only: the objective waypoint sprite is spawned, health-bar-updated, disowned-on-destroy, and ticked SERVER-SIDE (SpawnObjectiveSprite + WaypointSprites.Think; WaypointSprites.Active = Waypoints.cs:244). But there is NO client render/networking path — nothing in src/XonoticGodot.Net reads WaypointSprites to serialize/render it. So the attacker has no on-screen objective marker, health bar, or radar icon yet. The state machine is faithful; the visible pixels are absent.

[assault.mapobject.assault_wall | timing] func_assault_wall cosmetic wall toggles with its objective's health  -  Timing: QC re-evaluates each func_assault_wall on a 0.2s nextthink; the port re-evaluates every tick (DriveWalls from the per-frame DriveFrame) but only re-stamps the edict on a state transition. Functionally equivalent (toggle happens at most ~0.2s sooner); transition-gated and cheap. Not a behavioral divergence.

[assault.objective.destructible_heal | presentation] func_assault_destructible regen / event_heal (walls can be healed back up + sprite update)  -  Presentation: the heal updates the wall's waypoint-sprite health bar server-side (WaypointSprites.UpdateHealth) but, like all the Assault sprites, that bar is not surfaced to the client (no waypoint render/network path). QC's func_breakable_colormod (the visible color tint on a healed wall) is not modeled. The heal LOGIC + health restoration is faithful + live.

[assault.notify.play_hitsound | audio,liveness] Force a hitsound when shelling a func_assault_destructible (PlayHitsound hook)  -  The PlayHitsound handler is registered (Assault.cs:598-599) and its predicate matches QC exactly, but the hook chain is DEAD: MutatorHooks.FirePlayHitsound (MutatorHooks.cs:611) has ZERO callers. The port's hitsound is driven entirely by the STAT(HITSOUND_DAMAGE_DEALT_TOTAL) accumulator (DamageSystem.cs:337-343), which only accrues for IS_PLAYER victims and never consults the PlayHitsound mutator hook. So the assault forced-hitsound on a wall victim never actually plays. Behaviorally parity-neutral (a func_assault_destructible victim isn't a player, so the stat path wouldn't fire a hitsound either), but the code is unreachable.

[cl-hud.engine.registry_and_order | presentation] Panel registry, z-order, panel-order cvar  -  Draw order follows Godot child order, not the QC _hud_panelorder reverse-walk; relative panel layering may differ but panels don't overlap in luma.

[cl-hud.engine.skin_border | presentation] 9-slice skin border (draw_BorderPicture)  -  Directional border variants (_south/_north/_east/_corner_*) render as the full 9-slice frame rather than the engine's edge-masked crops; bg-enabled panels show a full frame where Base shows a partial edge.

[cl-hud.panel.radar | presentation,liveness] Radar / minimap panel (#6)  -  Maximized clickable mode (ons spawn / teleport select) is still unported; the discovered radar panel is force-hidden (StartHiddenIds) to avoid a double radar and the live radar is the standalone _radar.
[cl-hud.panel.pressedkeys | presentation] Pressed-keys panel (#11)  -  key_*/key_*_inv per-key art coverage not all runtime-verified (the inverted/pressed art set).

[cl-hud.panel.infomessages | logic] Info-messages panel (#14)  -  Cycling group hints (spectate controls), team-unbalanced nag, spectator list and the full message set are partially covered (the eliminated-LMS line is now present).
[cl-hud.panel.itemstime | presentation] Items-time panel (#17)  -  Progressbar / dynamicsize / ratio-columns fidelity not runtime-verified.
[cl-hud.panel.quickmenu | logic,presentation] Quick-menu panel  -  Nested quick-chat tree from a quickmenu file + translatecommands/server_is_default parity not verified.
[cl-hud.panel.strafehud | presentation] Strafe HUD panel  -  Bar zones / best-angle marker / wturn-switch indicator + mode/style/range fidelity not runtime-verified.

[cl-hud.panel.minigame | logic,presentation] Minigame HUD panels (BOARD/STATUS/HELP/MENU)  -  Board + status are merged into one MinigameRenderer (board square + team pieces + win-line + status line) rather than the QC BOARD/STATUS split; only the implemented minigame rule-sets are renderable. Per-game _hud_board/_hud_status art coverage not all runtime-verified.

[weapon-fireball.primary.launch | presentation] Primary launch: spawn slow large fireball (MOVETYPE_FLY, ±16, fire2 sound, muzzleflash)  -  No projectile flight sound (fireball_fly) / FIREBALL trail attached to the live plasma_prim entity (deferred CSQC render concern, same as other weapons).

[weapon-fireball.primary.explode | presentation] Fireball explode: heavy radius damage on impact/timeout  -  modeleffect_spawn(sphere.md3) BFG visual ball not produced (CSQC render layer); FIREBALL_EXPLODE + impact sound at ATTEN_NORM*0.25 are emitted.

[weapon-fireball.primary.bfg | presentation] BFG secondary blast: damage every visible enemy within bfgradius (distance-scaled, LOS-gated)  -  IS_INDEPENDENT_PLAYER(e) is checked but Entity.IsIndependentPlayer is a hardcoded stub (always false) — faithful for all non-independent (default) modes; the filter is a no-op until that mode is wired.; EFFECT_FIREBALL_BFGDAMAGE per-victim Emit is now wired, but its render fidelity (and the sphere.md3 ball) is the deferred CSQC layer.

[weapon-fireball.primary.laserscorch | presentation] Laser scorch: periodically set one nearby enemy alight (weighted nearest, prefer not-burning)  -  find is a plain radius (FindInRadius), not WarpZone_FindRadius (no warpzone-spanning scorch); same omission as all radius finds in the port.; EFFECT_FIREBALL_LASER scorch beam is Emit-wired (render fidelity deferred).

[weapon-fireball.primary.shootable | timing] Shootable fireball (event_damage destroys it; default health 0)  -  Depletion explode is SYNCHRONOUS (fired inside the damage call) rather than Base's deferred next-frame W_PrepareExplosionByDamage (nextthink=time; setthink), and the port does NOT call the MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(PrepareExplosionByDamage) hook. Shared Projectiles.MakeShootable simplification (Devastator/Electro/Hagar/Arc/Minelayer/Mortar/Seeker/OkRpc all share it). Only observable when a server raises g_balance_fireball_primary_health > 0 — at the stock health 0 the whole path is a faithful no-op. NOTE: the owner-reassign-to-attacker IS now ported (ShootDown gates it on g_projectiles_keep_owner==0, faithful to W_PrepareExplosionByDamage:94-98).; find/LaserPlay are still plain radius (warpzone-unaware) — unrelated to this row but the projectile world is non-warpzone like all port find

[weapon-fireball.secondary.firemine | presentation] Secondary: lob gravity bouncing firemine (MOVETYPE_BOUNCE, ±4, up-launch)  -  Port omits the bulletcounter%4 muzzle-corner f_diff offset + traceline nudge (cosmetic spawn-point spread of consecutive mines); all mines spawn from the same muzzle point. Unchanged from baseline.; No firemine flight sound / FIREMINE trail attached (deferred CSQC render).

[sv-campaign.welcome.levelnum | presentation] Campaign level number in the welcome/info message (Campaign_GetLevelNum)  -  Presentation surface differs from Base: Base's CSQC net_handle_ServerWelcome (client/main.qc:1379) builds a full Welcome MENU DIALOG; the port surfaces the same text as a single per-client CHAT line via Commands.ChatToPlayer (Campaign.Log fallback on a bare host). The level number, the per-level description (shortdesc + longdesc, sourced from the all-levels catalogue keyed by absolute level index), and the +jump keybind (resolved exactly like Base getcommandkey) are ALL reproduced — the only residual divergence is dialog-vs-chat. The 'no per-level description string' gap from the 2026-06-22 baseline is CLOSED (Wave 8e, commit 412ca19): CampaignLevelDescription (GameWorld.cs) now reconstructs the description the SVQC slice drops.

[mutator-sandbox.object.spawn | presentation] object_spawn — create object entity from a model in front of player  -  WIRED + LIVE: `g_sandbox` client-callable (ClientCommandRegistry:105) → CmdSandbox → HandleCommand; NetGame.WireSandbox assigns Trace (SandboxTraceProvider — real crosshair forward TraceService), so object_spawn lands at the trace endpoint with a real edict (Api.Entities.Spawn + SetModel + SetOrigin).; ObjectSpawn now DOES register the engine touch callback (e.Edict.Touch = ObjectTouch, SandboxMutator.cs:204) — the port equivalent of Base settouch (sv_sandbox.qc:180); see touch_material_fx row (now live). Think (setthink, sv_sandbox.qc:181) is driven externally by OnStartFrame over all objects rather than the per-edict engine think, which is faithful enough.; ModelExists() is permissive (returns true; no IModelService.fexists), so the Base non-existent-model rejection is

[mutator-sandbox.object.think_grab | presentation] Per-object think: grab-class assignment + owner UID resync + CSQC update  -  WIRED + LIVE: OnStartFrame is subscribed to MutatorHooks.SvStartFrame (fired in the server tick) and ticks ObjectThink for every object over the live RealClientsProvider roster (NetGame.WireSandbox — non-bot players); CryptoIdfpProvider(=PersistentId) resolves realowner from UID and clears it on disconnect.; Sandbox_DragAllowed veto hook ported and fired (MutatorHooks.FireSandboxDragAllowed, SandboxMutator.cs:346) — the grab-class branch is bit-faithful to Base (readonly OR DragAllowed→0). No subscriber exists, identical to Base's empty MUTATOR_CALLHOOK.; presentation: CSQCMODEL_AUTOUPDATE has no port seam (TODO SandboxMutator.cs:364); .Grab is computed but never consumed (no drag system).

[mutator-sandbox.object.edit_properties | presentation] object_edit — skin/alpha/color_main/color_glow/frame/scale/solidity/physics/force/material  -  WIRED + LIVE: object_edit reachable via CmdSandbox → HandleCommand on the live path.; physics-2 maps to MoveType.Physics (EntityEnums.cs:21, value 32) at SandboxMutator.cs:811, matching Base MOVETYPE_PHYSICS=32 (dpextensions.qc:1707) — the serialized movetype byte is Base-identical. (Behaviorally still think-only since the port has no ODE integrator, same as Base without ODE.); The faithful Base solidity→physics fall-through (missing break;) IS preserved (re-verified Base sv_sandbox.qc:685-697 has no break on solidity; port reproduces via goto case "physics" at SandboxMutator.cs:797).

[mutator-sandbox.object.scale | presentation] Object scale clamp + mins/maxs resize  -  WIRED + LIVE: reached from object_edit (live via CmdSandbox) and ObjectPortLoad (live via Store load/paste).; Model re-set + mins/maxs resize depend on the entity service (Edict); now that objects carry a real edict the SetModel/SetSize path executes.

[mutator-sandbox.object.attach | presentation] object_attach get/set/remove — child object attachment to tag/bone  -  WIRED + LIVE: object_attach get/set/remove reachable via CmdSandbox → HandleCommand.; presentation: setattachment (engine tag-follow) has no port seam (TODO SandboxMutator.cs:291) — attached children store the tag and old_solid/old_movetype faithfully but are not actually tag-followed.

[mutator-sandbox.object.duplicate | liveness] object_duplicate copy/paste via clipboard cvar  -  WIRED: copy/paste reachable via CmdSandbox → HandleCommand; PASTE is fully live (flood/cap/ObjectPortLoad).; COPY clipboard STILL inert: SandboxObjectStore.SetClipboard is an empty body (NetGame.cs:1046) — the port has no svc_stufftext channel to set a remote client's cvar. Paste from a manually-set cl_sandbox_clipboard works.; Paste shares the flood + maxobjects gates with object_spawn (faithful).

[mutator-sandbox.object.claim_info | presentation] object_claim + object_info object/mesh/attachments  -  WIRED + LIVE: claim/info reachable via CmdSandbox → HandleCommand; CryptoIdfpProvider(=PersistentId)/NetNameProvider/PrintTo(=SendChatToPlayer) assigned in NetGame.WireSandbox, so claim/owner-name/info-print all work.; object_info mesh: tag enumeration (FOR_EACH_TAG) needs the model-skeleton service — not implemented (TODO SandboxMutator.cs:876); mesh subcommand prints the header but no tag list.

[mutator-sandbox.drag.grab | logic,values,timing,presentation] Drag/grab integration: carry objects with +button8 gated by .grab  -  STILL MISSING: no Drag/Drag_Begin/Drag_Finish system. ObjectThink computes .Grab (e.Grab, written at SandboxMutator.cs:343-347) but nothing consumes it; +button8 does nothing; objects cannot be physically carried. (grep: no Drag_Begin/Drag_Finish/button8 grab consumer anywhere; e.Grab is written but never read.)

[weapon-tuba.reload.instrument_cycle | presentation] Reload cycles instrument Tuba -> Accordion -> Klein Bottle  -  weaponname (akordeon/kleinbottle) is not set — in Base it feeds only the CSQC wr_viewmodel (per-instrument view model h_akordeon/h_kleinbottle). The instrument index (TubaInstrument) IS tracked, so the obituary/sound branch correctly; only the per-instrument VIEW MODEL swap is unwired (render-only gap).

[weapon-tuba.note.sustain_lifetime | values,timing] Sustained note entity: spawn, refresh-on-hold, off-on-release, keep-alive  -  W_WeaponRateFactor scaling on the keep-alive — the port DOES multiply refire*2 by WeaponRateFactor(actor) (Tuba.cs:163), so this matches Base; only relevant under haste/rate modifiers.; No W_Tuba_NoteThink server-side per-listener volume/angle re-origin + SendFlags (moot until tuba-note sound networking exists). The port's NoteThink only models the teleport_time self-expiry, not the re-origin broadcast.

[weapon-tuba.fx.smoke_ring | presentation] Per-note smoke ring puff  -  Ring origin uses the player eye (Origin + ViewOfs) rather than the weapon muzzle tag (gettaginfo), so the puff spawns slightly off from Base — but Base itself FIXMEs that gettaginfo doesn't return the real tag origin (tuba.qc:309), so this is a near-equivalent approximation, not a faithful tag read. Per-instrument vertical offsets and the 0.25s throttle are bit-exact.

[weapon-tuba.audio.note_sound | values,timing,presentation] Sustained pitched note sound (loop sample, pitch step, fade, attenuation, networking)  -  No two-sample cos/sin crossfade: Base's in-range branch plays the floor sample at cos(pi/2 m/step) AND the upper neighbour at sin(...) for a smooth timbre blend; the port plays only the floor neighbour at full volume (residual PITCH is shifted via 2^(m/12), but the upper-neighbour timbre blend needs a second emitter and is unported).; No cl_tuba_fadetime release ramp: NoteOff Stops the loop cleanly rather than ramping volume down over 0.25s (needs a client-side per-frame volume think).; No ENT_CLIENT_TUBANOTE networking: remote players' tuba notes are not driven by a dedicated note entity (no per-listener re-origin / SendFlags broadcast).

[weapon-tuba.melody.recognition | presentation] Melody recognition + magic-ear chat (W_Tuba_HasPlayed / lastnotes)  -  The cosmetic 'NAME played on the @!#%'n Tuba/Accordeon/Klein Bottle: ...' bprint chat line on a recognised melody is NOT emitted — the magic-ear TRIGGER (UseTargets) fires, but the broadcast-print of the matched message text needs a bprint seam absent in Common (Tuba.NoteOff:213-214 notes this).

[weapon-tuba.identity.metadata | presentation] Weapon identity, flags, models, ammo, bot aim, registration/liveness  -  Per-instrument view models (h_akordeon/h_kleinbottle, driven by CSQC wr_viewmodel/weaponname) still unwired; render-only (see weapon-tuba.reload.instrument_cycle).; Crosshair gfx/crosshairtuba and weapon-image weapontuba assets exist in data but live-render wiring not verified (headless audit).

[cl-teamradar.links.onslaught | logic,values,timing,presentation] Onslaught radar links (control-point connection lines)  -  Onslaught radar links (ENT_CLIENT_RADARLINK / draw_teamradar_link / g_radarlinks) not networked or drawn — ONS radar shows control points but no connection lines.

[cl-teamradar.maximized.map | logic,values,timing,presentation] Maximized fullscreen tactical radar (m bind)  -  Maximized fullscreen radar entirely absent: no m-bind (+hud_panel_radar_maximized → cl_cmd hud radar 1), no fullscreen tactical overview, no separate maximized rotation/zoom/scale runtime, no default-border swap.

[cl-teamradar.maximized.click | logic,values,timing,presentation] Clickable radar: ONS spawn-point select + teleport, input/mouse/ESC  -  No clickable ONS spawn-point selection (cmd ons_spawn), no teleport-click, no mouse/key input handling (MOUSE1/MOUSE2/ESC/console-key passthrough), no dead+showscores temp-hide, no 'Click to select…' prompt, no icon hover-glow.

[cl-teamradar.cvar.zoomscale | logic,values,presentation] Zoom / scale blend (bigsize vs normalsize)  -  Zoom/scale uses a RangeUnits=2500 + scale/ScaleReferenceDivisor heuristic for the bigsize/normalsize endpoints, not the Base mi_scale/scale2d-derived sizes; the actual arena-fit big size is approximated by a fixed 2500u range.; Big-zoom recentering only happens when map bounds are loaded (HasMapBounds); the standalone path always has bounds, so this is live there, but the heuristic scale endpoints still differ from Base's exact fit.

[cl-teamradar.cvar.defaults | liveness] cvar registration + defaults / panel enable + visibility  -  Discovered HUD-registry radar is force-hidden (HudManager StartHiddenIds 'radar'), so only the standalone NetGame instance (256x256 at 24,24) is live — the enable/team gating runs against the standalone draw, which is correct, but the discovered panel never participates.; No hud_panel_radar_size cvar analogue (port keys off live control size + the 2500u range fallback instead of the 128px default).

[cl-teamradar.mutator.hook | logic,presentation] TeamRadar_Draw mutator hook (allow extra blips on radar)  -  No TeamRadar_Draw client mutator hook: mutators cannot add custom blips/icons to the radar, and the hook's return value (which Base ORs into the per-icon team-match gate for hover-glow visibility) is unmodeled.

[cl-teamradar.cvar.dynamichud | logic,presentation,liveness] dynamichud scaling toggle + cvar export to skin  -  hud_panel_radar_dynamichud is registered with the correct default (1) but nothing consumes it: the port has no HUD_Scale_Enable/Disable (the dynamic-HUD shrink applied when the player is alive) so the radar never participates in dynamic-HUD scaling.; HUD_Radar_Export (writing the radar's 8 aesthetic cvars into a saved hud skin file) has no port analogue; the port's skin/HUD-config export path does not round-trip these radar cvars.

[vehicle-racer.identity.def | presentation] Identity, hitbox, models, spawnflags  -  hud cockpit model + tag_viewport attachment and the scale-0.5 model hack are cosmetic-only and not instantiated (physics uses the real hitbox).; view_ofs '0 0 50' + height 200 (camera view offset/dist) not set; affects the cockpit camera, not the sim.

[vehicle-racer.spawn.lifecycle | presentation] vr_spawn / vr_setup: movetype, solid, health/shield/energy, regen capability flags, bounce/mass  -  scale 0.5 (cosmetic model-shrink hack) still not applied; physics uses the real hitbox so this is presentation-only.

[vehicle-racer.weapon.cannon | presentation] Primary energy laser cannon (rapid, energy-gated)  -  The EFFECT_RACER_MUZZLEFLASH muzzle effect is now emitted; the CSQCProjectile (PROJECTILE_WAKICANNON) bolt trail remains a client-render concern and is unported.

[vehicle-racer.weapon.rocket | presentation] Secondary rocket pair (lock-on / homing / ground-hugging)  -  The EFFECT_RACER_ROCKETLAUNCH launch effect is now emitted; the CSQCProjectile (PROJECTILE_WAKIROCKET) rocket trail remains a client-render concern and is unported.

[vehicle-racer.weapon.rockethud | presentation] Secondary HUD ammo/reload mirror (vehicle_ammo2 / vehicle_reload2)  -  Authority-side ammo2/reload2 % stats are written each tick onto the pilot; the client HUD progressbar that READS them (Vehicles_drawHUD vehicle_icon_ammo2) is presentation/CSQC and not yet wired — a client-render concern, not an authority gap.

[vehicle-bumblebee.spawn.vr_spawn | presentation] vr_spawn: sub-entities (gun1/gun2/gun3), flags, health/shield/movetype  -  Shield hit-flash entity (vehicle_shieldent, MDL_VEH_BUMBLEBEE_SHIELD, scale=512/vlen(maxs-mins)), cosmetic gun models (CANNON_RIGHT/LEFT/CENTER), cockpit/viewport model offsets (body hudmodel '50 0 -5'/viewport '5 0 2'; gun1 '90 -27 -23'/'-85 0 50'; gun2 '90 27 -23'/'-85 0 50'), scale 1.5, and the networked BRG_* raygun entity (Net_LinkEntity + bumble_raygun_send) are NOT created.

[vehicle-bumblebee.pilot.raygun | presentation] Center raygun: heal-beam (default) + damage-beam, target lock, energy gate  -  The visible BRG_* beam is a placeholder EffectEmitter.TeHealBeam/TeBeam stub (Bumblebee.cs:698-700), not the networked CSQC bumble_raygun beam; the per-tick BRG_START/BRG_END send + `wait = time + 1` beam-hold + the bumble_raygun_draw multi-segment cylinder are not modeled. Beam COLOUR is bit-faithful (pure green heal / pure red damage).

[vehicle-bumblebee.gunner.frame | presentation] Side-gunner turret: per-gun lock + lead + aim + plasma fire  -  Gunner aux crosshairs + cockpit HUD are now LIVE (Wave-7): GunnerFrame writes VehGunnerLeadPoint/Valid + VehGunnerHitPoint/Valid (Bumblebee.cs:605/648) and mirrors gunner.VehicleHealth/Shield/Ammo1/Energy (632-636); NetGame.UpdateVehicleHud selects the BumblebeeGun art for a seated gunner and projects the magenta '1 0 1' LEAD (aux slot 1) + reload-color READY (aux slot 0) crosshairs, AND mirrors the gunner READY markers onto the PILOT (slots 1/2 via FeedPilotGunnerAux). RESIDUAL presentation: cosmetic gun cockpit models + viewport offsets are still not built (HUD bars/crosshairs render, but the 3D gun cockpit art does not).

[vehicle-bumblebee.gunner.cannon | presentation] Side-gunner plasma cannon projectile (bumblebee_fire_cannon)  -  Wave-8e FIX: EFFECT_BIGPLASMA_MUZZLEFLASH is now emitted at the muzzle (FireCannon: EffectEmitter.Emit('BIGPLASMA_MUZZLEFLASH', org, vel, 1); BIGPLASMA_MUZZLEFLASH registered EffectsList.cs:163). RESIDUAL: the networked CSQCProjectile (PROJECTILE_BUMBLE_GUN) bolt trail/model visual is still NOT IMPLEMENTED.

[vehicle-bumblebee.death | presentation] Death: eject, gib toss, blowup, respawn  -  Gib toss (gun1/gun2/gun3 from their tags + body via vehicle_tossgib, scale 1.5) NOT IMPLEMENTED — the vehicle_gib physics-entity subsystem is unported.

[vehicle-bumblebee.client.presentation | logic,values,timing,presentation,liveness] Client presentation: BRG beam, HUD, crosshairs, models, describe  -  The networked BRG_* healray/damage beam (the signature green/red ray; ENT_CLIENT_BUMBLE_RAYGUN, bumble_raygun_send/NET_HANDLE) + bumble_raygun_draw multi-segment Draw_CylindricLine cylinder NOT IMPLEMENTED (placeholder EffectEmitter.TeHealBeam/TeBeam stub only; colour bit-faithful).; Wave-7 LIVE NOW (in addition to the Wave-5 pilot HUD): the GUNNER cockpit HUD (VehicleHudKind.BumblebeeGun selected for a seated gunner, NetGame.cs:3001) + the two GUNNER aux crosshairs (magenta '1 0 1' lead + reload-color READY, NetGame.cs:3025-3034) + the PILOT mirror of both gunners' READY markers (FeedPilotGunnerAux, NetGame.cs:3050-3051) all render each frame. STILL missing: the 'No right/left gunner!' blinking prompts (vr_hud text drawn when AuxiliaryX

[monster-golem.attack.lightning | presentation] Ranged lightning chunk: bouncing shootable projectile, 5s fuse  -  Projectile renders the correct model (models/ebomb.mdl) but at 1x — QC gren.scale = 2.5 (golem.qc:145) is not networked (Entity has no render-scale field; the CSQCMODEL scale-bit contract is unported). Visible bolt is smaller than Base but no longer headless.

[monster-golem.audio.voice_cues | audio] Monster_Sound voice cues (idle/sight/melee/spawn/pain/death) + antilag throttle  -  Full data-driven .sounds-file parse + skin-keyed Monster_Sounds_Update is still hardcoded per-descriptor (SoundCues set + SoundCueCount switch mirror golem.dpm_0.sounds by hand) rather than parsed from the model's .sounds at load; behaviorally faithful for golem but the general GlobalSound/Monster_Sounds_Update plumbing is a named no-op.; QC spamsound(SND_BODYIMPACT1) pain placeholder (when a monster has no pain sample) not played — n/a for golem (golem has pain samples).

[monster-golem.anim_frames | timing,presentation] Animation frame-group table (mr_anim) for .dpm playback  -  AnimFrame stamps only the group START index onto Entity.Frame (server-side, per think via DriveAnimFrame). The animfixfps framecount/fps per-frame playback advance WITHIN a group is not driven — there is no true .dpm frame timeline; the client renders a single stamped frame per phase/group (timing/presentation partial).

[monster-golem.menu.describe | presentation] Monsterpedia describe() flavor text (MENUQC)  -  MENUQC describe() returns the golem's 3-paragraph monsterpedia entry; the port has no monster info-page system, so this text is not surfaced. Pure menu/UI flavor, no gameplay effect.

[cl-viewmodel.anim.networked_frames | logic,values,timing,presentation,liveness] Networked weapon fire/idle/reload animation (wframe temp entity)  -  No networked weapon fire/idle/reload animation (the QC wframe temp entity is not networked); the gun never visibly cycles or reloads.

[cl-viewmodel.model.skin_colormap_glow_effects | values,presentation] Per-child skin / team colormap / glowmod / EF_NODEPTHTEST / cheap effects mask  -  Team colormap is an approximate whole-model albedo tint (0.6 + 0.4*teamColor), not Base's true colormap palette region swap (256 + c); reads as your team color but is not pixel-faithful.; Per-child m_skin (wepskin) not applied; the gun renders with its base material.

[cl-viewmodel.align.gunalign_gunoffset | values] Shot-origin alignment (cl_gunalign 1/2/3/4) + cl_gunoffset  -  cl_gunalign side magnitude is a fixed representative AuthoredShotSideY (-3.5u) rather than each v_ model's own shot-tag vecs.y; left/center now move the gun but by an approximate amount, not the per-model Base value.

[cl-viewmodel.muzzle.flash_particle | values,presentation] Muzzle flash particle (local firer + remote world-space, m_muzzleeffect)  -  Local muzzle position is a heuristic at the tag_shot socket rather than Base's view-projected movedir_aligned (not verified to read identically on-screen).; Secondary fire does not flash (only the primary _attackHeld path calls Fire; UpdateLocalFireFeedback comments 'secondary FX stay networked for now').; ClientWorld.OnMuzzleFlash wrapper has no caller (NetGame calls _viewModel.Fire() directly) — dead wrapper, but the feature is live by another path.

[cl-viewmodel.casings.brass_eject | timing] Brass / shell casings (eject, tumble, bounce, fade, bounce sounds)  -  Casing physics is a self-integrated gravity + single ground-plane bounce (FloorZ), not full MOVETYPE_BOUNCE world collision at cl_casings_ticrate (0.03125); on most maps no floorZ is supplied so the casing falls through and only the lifetime fade culls it.

[turret-fusionreactor.identity.def | presentation] Identity / model / hitbox / support class flags  -  head_model (reactor.md3) is not carried — no head entity exists in the port turret model.; TUR_FLAG_SUPPORT/AMMOSOURCE are not stored as flags; support behavior is hardcoded in the C# class instead of derived from spawnflags. (Verified TUR_FLAG_AMMOSOURCE is declared but NEVER read anywhere in Base qcsrc — purely declarative — so its omission is functionally harmless; TUR_FLAG_SUPPORT only selects turret_targetscore_support, which the reactor never invokes since it has no targetscore-driven path.)

[turret-fusionreactor.setup.tr_setup | presentation] Setup flags: energy+recharge ammo, own-team range-limited targeting, HITALLVALID, no aim/track, head spin seed  -  Initial head spin (50 deg/s) and head scale 0.75 are not applied — no head entity / no client render.

[turret-fusionreactor.fx.attack | values,presentation,liveness] te_smallflash at recharged recipient + ammo-scaled head spin  -  No client turret render (ENT_CLIENT_TURRET): the reactor renders no model and no head, so the ammo-scaled head spin (Base '0 250 0'*ammo/ammo_max, up to 250 deg/s) is computed onto TurretState.HeadAVelocity but never integrated/displayed. No waypoint sprite/healthbar/death FX either.

[turret-fusionreactor.menu.describe | logic,presentation] MENUQC turret-codex help text (describe)  -  The MENUQC describe() help string (shown in the menu turret codex) is not ported — the port has no menu turret-description surface for the Fusion Reactor.

[cl-view.chase.classic_thirdperson | logic] Classic chase_active third-person + spectator camera (chase_back/up/front/overhead; View_SpectatorCamera)  -  The server-side spectator-camera mode toggle is not ported: View_SpectatorCamera's STAT(CAMERA_SPECTATOR) 1/2 state + IMP_weapon_drop cycling (the chase_active<=0 auto-switch that flips chase_active to -2 and Impulse_Sends weapon-drop to cycle the server spectator camera) has no port analogue. The port drives the spectator third-person purely from the user chase_active cvar.

[cl-view.demo.free_camera_lockview_ortho | logic,values,timing,presentation] Demo free camera + lockview + orthoview + viewloc + FPS report  -  No demo free/chase camera (CSQC_Demo_Camera), no view lock (cl_lockview), no orthographic radar-render view (cl_orthoview / View_Ortho), no viewloc 2.5D side-scroller camera (GetViewLocationFOV), and no FPS report to the server (fpscounter_update STAT(SHOWFPS)).

[turret-plasma.identity.model_hitbox | presentation] Identity, hitbox, model, health, weapon binding  -  head_model (plasma.md3/plasmad.md3) is never loaded or rendered — there is no tur_head sub-entity, only the base body.

[turret-plasma.track.fluid_inertia | presentation] Head track motor (fluid-inertia) with per-axis pitch/rot clamps  -  Head angles live only in server TurretState and are never networked/rendered — the head never visibly aims (presentation).

[turret-plasma.headspin.frame_anim | presentation] Head spin frame animation after each shot (tr_think frame 1->5)  -  PRESENTATION only: the frame-cycle logic runs server-side on the turret edict's e.Frame, but the body is a static base.md3 with no head-spin frames and there is no rendered tur_head sub-entity / CSQC turret net layer — so the spin is computed but never visibly shown.

[turret-plasma.death.die_respawn | presentation] Death + respawn (turret_die / turret_hide / turret_respawn)  -  Death client gibs (base-gib1..4 + head gib toss/turret_gibboom) are still not spawned — needs a CSQC turret edict. The explosion + impact sound ARE now emitted.

[cl-shownames.gate.self_spectatee | presentation,liveness] Own-tag in chase + spectatee-switch window  -  Chase own-tag for the truly-local player still can't draw: the predicted local player is never in ClientNet.RemoteIds (HandleSnapshot skips LocalNetId), so the self branch only fires for the spectatee. In stock play this matters only for hud_shownames_self + chase_active on your OWN model in third person — a residual deferral; the spectatee path (the common observed case) is live.

[vehicle-spiderbot.identity.registration | presentation] Spiderbot vehicle type registration + identity (model, name, hitbox, view)  -  view_ofs '0 0 70' / height 170 (the seated camera offset + chase distance) are client-view concerns not modeled on the headless server descriptor.

[vehicle-spiderbot.weapon.minigun | presentation] Twin alternating-barrel hitscan miniguns (heat/ammo belt, spread, solid penetration)  -  Cosmetic barrel spin (QC gun1.angles.z += 45 / gun2.angles.z -= 45 with 360 wrap) is done client-side (Firing flag in VehicleVisuals); the server-state field is not advanced — functionally fine, non-1:1 split.; FIXED-SINCE-BASELINE: EFFECT_SPIDERBOT_MINIGUN_MUZZLEFLASH is now emitted server-side via EffectEmitter.Emit (Spiderbot.cs:493; effect registered src/XonoticGodot.Common/Gameplay/Effects/EffectsList.cs:161), matching QC spiderbot.qc:276 Send_Effect(EFFECT_SPIDERBOT_MINIGUN_MUZZLEFLASH, v, v_forward*2500, 1). (The client VehicleVisuals Firing flag also pops a flash; the server emit is the QC-faithful path.) Residual presentation gap = barrel-spin server-state only.

[vehicle-spiderbot.weapon.rockets | presentation] 3-mode rocket launcher (VOLLY salvo / GUIDE homing / ARTILLERY lob) + belt reload  -  ARTILLERY clearance-height traces use a headless WorldOnly trace; the h1/h2 (0.75 of the up/diagonal world clearance) are reproduced but the resulting arc was not in-game verified.; FIXED-SINCE-BASELINE: the launch effect EFFECT_SPIDERBOT_ROCKETLAUNCH is now emitted server-side via EffectEmitter.Emit (Spiderbot.cs:629; effect registered src/XonoticGodot.Common/Gameplay/Effects/EffectsList.cs:157), matching QC vehicles_projectile Send_Effect. Residual: the rocket-in-flight CSQCProjectile visual (PROJECTILE_SPIDERROCKET) stays client/net deferred to the shared projectile catalog.

[vehicle-spiderbot.death.blowup | presentation] vr_death + spiderbot_blowup (burn, gib entities, 250-dmg blast, respawn)  -  Presentation still simplified: no 4 physics gib entities (head MF_ROCKET+EF_FLAME bounce+fade via spiderbot_headfade, 2 tossed guns body-vel v_forward*700, body frame 11). FIXED-SINCE-BASELINE: the per-burn EFFECT_EXPLOSION_SMALL (Spiderbot.cs:459) and the spiderbot_headfade EFFECT_EXPLOSION_BIG (Spiderbot.cs:440) are now emitted server-side via EffectEmitter.Emit (the Send_Effect equivalent; effect names registered EffectsList.cs:29/31). The head EF_FLAME flag + the physics gib bodies stay a TODO (client VehicleVisuals does an approximate scripted gib arc).

[monster-mage.attack.spike | presentation] Homing electric spike (one in flight, seeker guidance)  -  CSQC spike projectile model (PROJECTILE_MAGE_SPIKE, mage.qc:238 CSQCProjectile) still not wired — SpawnProjectile sets no projectile visual/ProjectileType, so the spike has no client-visible model/trail in flight (cf. Spider.cs which DOES set PROJECTILE_ELECTRO).

[monster-mage.defend.heal | presentation] Radius heal of self/allies (skin variants: health/ammo/armor)  -  Healed-ally waypoint-sprite health update (WaypointSprite_UpdateHealth, mage.qc:280-281) not invoked in the port — no WaypointSprite_UpdateHealth/UpdateHealth call anywhere in Gameplay/Monsters.

[monster-mage.menu.describe | presentation,liveness] MENUQC monster-tooltip description text  -  Mage.Describe() (Mage.cs:86-93) is the verbatim bestiary prose but has NO caller: the only references to Describe() are the base virtual (EntityClasses.cs:106) and unrelated DPM/DeferredCommands.Describe overloads. No bestiary/monster-tooltip UI surface in the port renders descriptor Describe() text, so the prose never reaches the screen.

[turret-ewheel.identity.def | presentation] Identity, hitbox, models, spawnflags  -  Head model (ewheel-gun1.md3) not instantiated as a separate head entity; no tag_head attachment.; ROAM spawnflag not modeled as an explicit flag (the path-roaming behavior it gates is now implemented and live — see turret-ewheel.drive.path).

[turret-ewheel.weapon.fire | presentation] EWheelAttack: fast blaster bolt volley (2 shots, near-hitscan)  -  No two-cannon head-frame alternation (Base ewheel_weapon.qc:32-35: tur_head.frame += 2, wrap > 3 -> 0) — needs the unported head sub-entity (the turret's own Entity.Frame is consumed by the locomotion drive animation); tracked under turret-ewheel.identity.def.; EFFECT_BLASTER_MUZZLEFLASH is emitted with except: turret, but Base Send_Effect -> Send_Effect_Except(..., NULL) does NOT exclude the firer — minor over-exclusion (cosmetic for a turret, which is not a first-person view source).

[turret-ewheel.client.draw | presentation] CSQC rolling draw + low-health sparks (ewheel_draw)  -  CSQC ewheel_draw rolling integration not ported: the model does not client-side roll (origin += velocity*dt, head angles += avelocity*dt, MOVETYPE_BOUNCE+gravity). The server already networks origin/velocity, so the visible motion follows the netted transform rather than the client integration.
